text: Polynesian navigators settled the vast Pacific Ocean (covering 1/3 of Earth's surface) using wayfinding — navigation by stars, ocean swells, wind patterns, bird behavior, and clouds. They reached Hawaii, Easter Island (Rapa Nui), and New Zealand (Aotearoa) — the last uninhabited landmasses on Earth settled by humans (~1200 CE).
Wayfinding: memorized star compass (32+ points), read reflected/refracted swells, observed birds (landfinding species: terns, frigates).
Austronesian migration: started from Taiwan ~3000 BCE → Philippines → Indonesia → Pacific (Lapita culture) → Remote Oceania.
Hokule'a: modern replica that sailed Hawaii to Tahiti (1976) proving ancient methods.
